prompt:
Before solving this problem, analyze it:
(a) What type of problem is this?
(b) What cognitive strategy is most relevant?
(c) What will the hardest part be?
Then solve it.

Respond ONLY with JSON:
{"problem_type": "...", "strategy": "...", "hardest_part": "...", "solution": "..."}

Problem: A hospital needs to schedule 4 surgeons across 3 operating rooms over
5 days. Each surgeon can perform at most 2 surgeries per day. There are
15 scheduled surgeries with the following constraints: Surgeries S1-S5
require Surgeon A or B (specialized). Surgeries S6-S10 can be done by
any surgeon. Surgeries S11-S15 require Surgeon C or D. Surgery S3 and
S7 need the same operating room (shared specialized equipment) but cannot
happen on the same day. Surgery S12 must happen before S14 (staged
procedure). No operating room can host more than 2 surgeries per day.
Provide a valid schedule or prove it is impossible.

parsed.problem_type: Constraint Satisfaction Problem (CSP) with temporal and resource allocation constraints
parsed.strategy: Decompose into constraint categories → apply constraint propagation → systematically assign with backtracking search → verify dependency and capacity constraints
parsed.hardest_part: The interaction between the shared equipment constraint (S3 & S7 must use same room but different days), surgeon specialization bottlenecks (10 surgeries require specific pairs A/B or C/D), and capacity limits (2 per room/day, 2 per surgeon/day) creates a tightly coupled system where early assignments can block later ones

constraint_verification.surgeon_specialization: ✓ S1-S5 (A|B): 2A, 3B assigned | S11-S15 (C|D): 3C, 2D assigned | S6-S10 flexible: distributed
constraint_verification.shared_equipment: ✓ S3 (Room 1, Day 2) and S7 (Room 1, Day 3) share Room 1, different days
